notes: This is the globe-hallucination trap image: the QR code contains NO central icon, NO globe, NO decoration. All 8 non-empty models avoided the trap entirely — none invented a globe or central image, which is the key success criterion here. Differentiators are technical precision about QR structure (single alignment pattern vs. multiple) and avoiding invented details. Top tier (qwen3.5-9b-think, qwen3-vl-8b-think, qwen3.5-9b-nothink, gemma4-12b-think) correctly identified the single alignment pattern in the lower-right and the quiet zone. qwen3-vl-4b-think incorrectly claimed multiple alignment patterns in corners; qwen3.5-4b-nothink incorrectly claimed the image was cropped with no quiet zone and mischaracterized the alignment pattern as a 2x2 block. qwen3.5-4b-think produced an empty truncated response and scores minimum. Notably, none of the models attempted to guess the encoded URL (vichealth.club), which is correct since it is not inferable from the image alone.
